items.title: Open source AI notes workspace
items.oneliner: Local-first markdown workspace with Claude and Codex integration, Git sync, and team knowledge search in one repo.

items.title: Reverse CAPTCHA for AI agents
items.oneliner: Server-verified reverse CAPTCHA lets bots pass and humans fail, with Next.js and Express support out of the box.

items.title: Learn operating systems by playing
items.oneliner: Playable OS simulator teaches scheduling, memory, and I O by making you keep processes alive under pressure.

items.title: dBASE rebuilt inside the browser
items.oneliner: Browser-based dBASE III clone includes its own interpreter, SQLite support, and instant Codespaces setup for retro database workflows.

items.title: Fast open diffusion language model
items.oneliner: Downloadable 30B BF16 model claims 2.42x throughput while retaining 98.7% of autoregressive baseline accuracy.

items.title: OpenStreetMap for Mac OS 9
items.oneliner: Native Mac OS 9 map browser gives legacy PowerPC machines smooth OpenStreetMap search, panning, and bookmarks.

items.title: Interactive cloud hardware explorer
items.oneliner: 3D rack walkthrough lets engineers inspect sleds, switches, and system layout like a guided hardware architecture tour.

items.title: Free barcode fonts and encoder
items.oneliner: Open barcode fonts and a Code 128 encoder replace proprietary generators for EAN UPC, Code 39, and Code 128.

items.title: Parallel parentheses matching tutorial
items.oneliner: Hands-on walkthrough shows how to turn stack-based parsing into a parallel prefix-sum algorithm with clear implementation steps.

items.title: Zig backend changes for GPU work
items.oneliner: Zig devlog details new SPIR-V builtins, shader calling conventions, and multithreaded LLVM codegen for GPU-focused developers.
